title: austroderia toetoe
intro: Austroderia toetoe, also known as Toetoe or Red Tussock, is a native New Zealand grass that belongs to the Poaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been observed in its ability to treat wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Austroderia toetoe include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory conditions such as bronchitis and asthma. In traditional Maori herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or infusion by steeping the leaves and stems in hot water, and it is also used in topical applications such as creams and ointments to harness its medicinal properties.
